metadata.objective: Create a clear 90-minute lecture for first-year students

summary: Prepare a weekly lecture is slowed mainly by search old folders and gather source materials, draft slides and speaker notes, recreate a lecture outline from source material. The redesign adds repeatable structure while preserving human judgment.

diagnosis.description: Search old folders and gather source materials
diagnosis.recommendation: Use the same location, naming rule, and sequence every time.

diagnosis.description: Review the syllabus and define learning objectives
diagnosis.recommendation: Keep the decision with the user and provide a review checklist.

diagnosis.description: Recreate a lecture outline from source material
diagnosis.recommendation: Keep the decision with the user and provide a review checklist.
Let AI create a first pass, then require a named human review.
Let AI create a first pass, then require a named human review.

diagnosis.description: Review academic accuracy and teaching quality
diagnosis.recommendation: Keep the decision with the user and provide a review checklist.

priorityImprovements.description: Search old folders and gather source materials
priorityImprovements.recommendation: Use the same location, naming rule, and sequence every time.
Let AI create a first pass, then require a named human review.

priorityImprovements.description: Recreate a lecture outline from source material
priorityImprovements.recommendation: Keep the decision with the user and provide a review checklist.

improvedWorkflow.title: Create a standardized session workspace
improvedWorkflow.input: Course code, week, and session topic
improvedWorkflow.action: Create the session folder from the standard structure and apply the naming rule.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: None; this is a rule-based setup step.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: Confirm the course, week, and access permissions.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if the session identity or approved storage location is unclear.

improvedWorkflow.title: Collect source materials
improvedWorkflow.input: Syllabus, previous lecture, textbook chapter, and approved references
improvedWorkflow.action: Copy or link the required sources into the session workspace and record gaps.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may summarize the manifest only after the professor supplies approved sources.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: Confirm every source is relevant and permitted for use.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if a required source is missing or its provenance is unknown.

improvedWorkflow.title: Define learning objectives
improvedWorkflow.action: Complete the learning-objective template with observable outcomes and scope boundaries.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may propose wording; it may not decide the teaching scope.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: The professor approves objectives and alignment with the course.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if objectives cannot be tied to the syllabus.

improvedWorkflow.title: Generate the lecture brief
improvedWorkflow.action: Run the lecture-brief instruction to draft prerequisites, concepts, likely misconceptions, and evidence to verify.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI creates a source-grounded first draft and flags uncertainty.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: Verify every academic claim and remove material outside the course level.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if the draft introduces unsupported claims.

improvedWorkflow.title: Create the lesson structure
improvedWorkflow.action: Allocate time to opening, explanation, examples, practice, discussion, and close.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may suggest an initial sequence; the professor selects the pedagogy.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: Confirm pacing, emphasis, and teaching sequence.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if the plan exceeds the session length.

improvedWorkflow.title: Plan slides
improvedWorkflow.input: Timed lesson structure and source manifest
improvedWorkflow.action: Complete the slide-planning template with one purpose and evidence source per slide group.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may draft concise headings and notes from approved material.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: Review accuracy, accessibility, and cognitive load.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if a slide cannot be traced to an approved source or objective.

improvedWorkflow.title: Create an activity or discussion
improvedWorkflow.action: Use the activity template to define instructions, timing, expected response, and debrief.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may propose variants; it must not evaluate identifiable students.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: The professor approves feasibility, inclusivity, and alignment.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if the activity does not support an objective.

improvedWorkflow.title: Adapt material to the student level
improvedWorkflow.input: Slide plan, activity, and cohort context
improvedWorkflow.action: Run the student-level checklist and revise terminology, examples, scaffolding, and assumed knowledge.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may flag jargon or missing explanations without using student records.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: The professor decides what is appropriate for the cohort.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if adaptation would require identifiable student information.

improvedWorkflow.title: Review academic accuracy
improvedWorkflow.action: Verify claims, citations, examples, calculations, and distinctions between fact and interpretation.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may produce a review checklist but cannot approve content.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: The professor performs and records final academic review.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop and return to the relevant step when a claim cannot be verified.

improvedWorkflow.title: Export the final teaching package
improvedWorkflow.action: Export slides, notes, activity instructions, and source manifest using the naming convention.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: None; export uses a standard checklist.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: Confirm files open correctly and only intended materials are shared.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if permissions or exported files cannot be verified.

improvedWorkflow.title: Complete a post-class reflection
improvedWorkflow.action: Record what worked, timing variance, misconceptions, and one change for the next run.
improvedWorkflow.aiInvolvement: AI may later summarize non-sensitive reflections across sessions.
improvedWorkflow.humanReview: The professor decides which changes should carry forward.
improvedWorkflow.failureCondition: Stop if reflection notes would expose identifiable student information.

assets.name: Lecture-brief instruction
assets.content: Using only the supplied approved sources and objectives, draft prerequisites, core concepts, likely misconceptions, and terms to explain. Cite the supplied source for each academic claim. Mark missing evidence as [VERIFY]. Do not infer student characteristics. Return a draft for professor review.
assets.purpose: Create a bounded first draft without surrendering the professor's judgment.
assets.humanVerification: A person must verify the draft against approved inputs before using it.
assets.prohibitedUse: Do not include confidential or identifiable student information, invent evidence, or treat the draft as approved academic work.

exampleExecution.scenario: Prepare the next first-year lecture using approved syllabus outcomes and one textbook chapter.
observations: Illustrative run: workspace and approved-source manifest were ready in 14 minutes.
The AI-assisted lecture brief contained two claims marked [VERIFY]; the professor corrected both before slide planning.
Illustrative total: 132 minutes, compared with the 240-minute user estimate; this is an example, not a measured product claim.
The professor completed the academic review and retained all final teaching decisions.

riskNotes: Review AI-assisted output for accuracy before use.
Do not include student records or identifiable student work in external AI tools.
Workflow Lab guides the sequence but does not operate external tools or make accountable academic decisions.

qualityIndicators: Human review finds no critical errors
The user can repeat the workflow without assistance
reviewQuestions: How many minutes did each step and the full workflow take?
How many factual, structural, or scheduling corrections were required?
Where did you stop, become uncertain, or need operator help?
Rate the effort required from 1 (very low) to 5 (very high).
Could you repeat the workflow next time without assistance? Why or why not?
Which reusable asset helped, and what should change before the next run?
